skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Reviews frontend files against Vercel Web Interface Guidelines, accessibility expectations, and UX best practices. It fetches the latest guideline document before each review.
skill.summary: Audit UI code against current web interface and accessibility guidelines.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, weak-crypto, and path-traversal alerts are false positives caused by Markdown syntax, tags, and relative documentation links. The confirmed concern is that the skill requires fetching and applying unpinned remote guideline text, which can change outside marketplace review and may influence agent behavior.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Remote Guideline Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to fetch fresh guideline content from a raw GitHub URL and apply all rules from that remote content. This is useful for freshness, but the remote instructions can change after marketplace review and could introduce prompt injection or unsafe agent behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency and instruction to apply fetched rules are explicit. I did not find evidence that the current URL is malicious, so the concern is supply-chain and prompt-injection exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Markdown Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged code fences, inline code examples, and tag formatting as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The files contain skill documentation only, with no executable script, command runner, or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples and Markdown formatting. No executable language file or runtime invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited content is frontmatter, descriptive text, usage labels, and Markdown tags.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains no hashing, encryption, signing, or credential-handling logic. The weak-crypto labels appear to come from token matches in prose.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to related-skill links using parent-directory syntax. These are Markdown references, not filesystem operations or instructions to read arbitrary parent directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are link targets in a related-skills section. I found no evidence that the skill executes those paths or uses them to access files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Prompt Content Controls Review Rules
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent that fetched remote content contains all rules and output format instructions. Treating remote text as authoritative instructions creates a prompt-injection surface if the upstream content changes or is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described, but no malicious remote payload is present in the reviewed skill files. The risk depends on future upstream content and agent isolation.

content.user_title: Audit UI Code Against Web Guidelines
content.value_statement: Frontend teams need consistent checks for accessibility, layout quality, and interface polish. This skill reviews selected files against current Vercel Web Interface Guidelines and reports actionable issues.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the current Vercel Web Interface Guidelines before each review.
Reviews React, Vue, Svelte, HTML, CSS, SCSS, JavaScript, and TypeScript files.
Checks UI code for accessibility, UX, and interface guideline violations.
Reports findings with file and line references when source locations are available.
Suggests fixes for reported UI and accessibility issues.
limitations: Depends on an external guideline URL that can change over time.
Does not run browser automation or visual regression tests by itself.
Review quality depends on the files and patterns the user provides.
May miss runtime-only issues that are not visible in source files.

use_cases.title: Component Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review changed UI components before merge and flag accessibility or interface consistency issues.

use_cases.title: Design QA Pass
use_cases.description: Check implemented pages against practical web interface rules before stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Triage
use_cases.description: Scan selected files for common accessibility gaps and prioritize fixes with clear source locations.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my UI code in src/components/Button.tsx against the web design guidelines. Report issues with file and line references.

prompt_templates.title: Check Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Check accessibility issues in src/components/**/*.tsx. Prioritize keyboard support, semantic elements, labels, and focus states.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Page Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for a feature area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the checkout UI files in src/app/checkout for UX, accessibility, and guideline compliance. Include fixes for each finding.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Release Candidate
prompt_templates.prompt: Review src/app and src/components for launch readiness against the latest web interface guidelines. Group findings by severity and include practical remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my UI code in the button component.
output_examples.output: The review identifies missing accessible names on icon-only buttons, weak focus indicators, and inconsistent disabled-state styling. Each issue includes the affected file, line, severity, and a suggested fix.
The review flags missing focus trapping, incomplete escape-key behavior, and missing dialog labels. The response prioritizes keyboard-blocking issues before visual polish items.

output_examples.input: Audit the landing page before release.
output_examples.output: The review reports layout, semantic structure, responsive behavior, and accessibility concerns. It separates launch-blocking issues from polish improvements.

best_practices: Review a focused file set so findings remain specific and actionable.
Verify remote guideline content before applying recommendations in sensitive repositories.
Pair the review with browser testing for runtime behavior and visual states.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad repository-wide reviews when only one feature changed.
Do not treat guideline findings as a substitute for manual accessibility testing.
Do not blindly follow remote guideline text if it contains unrelated agent instructions.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews frontend source files for web interface, UX, and accessibility guideline issues.

faq.question: Does it modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to report findings and suggest fixes unless the user asks for edits.

faq.question: Why does it use the network?
faq.answer: It fetches the latest Vercel Web Interface Guidelines before each review.

faq.question: Can it review an entire application?
faq.answer: Yes, but smaller file groups produce clearer findings and reduce context limits.

faq.question: Does it replace automated accessibility tests?
faq.answer: No. It complements tests by reviewing source code and suggesting improvements.

faq.question: What should users verify before using it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the fetched guideline source is trusted and relevant to their project.
